raw_consumer_statement:
- Purchased XXXX smart toilets and a XXXX appliance for {$940.00} on XX/XX/year> - Merchant failed to deliver the goods - Merchant admitted by text message that the goods could not be shipped - Merchant directed me to request a refund through Citi - I submitted a written dispute via fax per Citis instructions on XX/XX/XXXX including receipt of payment on XX/XX/XXXX and statement reflecting purchase on XX/XX/XXXX. 
- Citi acknowledged receipt twice by email - Citi failed to open an official FCBA dispute due solely to internal timing - No dispute ID was ever provided -Citi opened my dispute incorrectly as a fraud dispute even though I told them repeatedly by phone that I was NOT reporting fraud. I clearly stated this was a billing error involving merchandise that the merchant could not deliver. Citi misclassified the dispute, then refused to help because they said I was outside the 90-day window. This misclassification and refusal violate the Fair Credit Billing Act.
